pattern: Swapping indices two apart can only ever shuffle characters within the even positions or within the odd positions, so the two strings can be made equal exactly when their even-position multisets match and their odd-position multisets match.
hints: A swap between indices that differ by exactly two never moves a character from an even index to an odd index or vice versa.
That means the even-indexed characters can be freely rearranged among themselves, and separately the odd-indexed characters can be freely rearranged among themselves.
So the strings are reachable from each other exactly when the multiset of characters at even positions match between the two strings, and likewise for the odd positions.

edge_inputs.description: reachable through swaps within each parity class
not reachable, the parity multisets differ
same overall letters but an even-position letter would need to land on an odd position
even positions match but odd positions do not

stress.description: random length-four lowercase strings, sometimes built to be reachable and sometimes not
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    s1 = ''.join(chr(97 + rng.randint(0, 4)) for _ in range(4))
    if rng.random() < 0.5:
        evens = [s1[0], s1[2]]
        odds = [s1[1], s1[3]]
        rng.shuffle(evens)
        rng.shuffle(odds)
        s2 = evens[0] + odds[0] + evens[1] + odds[1]
    else:
        s2 = ''.join(chr(97 + rng.randint(0, 4)) for _ in range(4))
    return [s1, s2]
stress.note: half the cases are constructed to be reachable, half are fully random

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def canBeEqual(self, s1, s2):
        # Independent implementation: brute-force every reachable permutation
        # of s1 by applying the two allowed swaps repeatedly to a fixed point,
        # instead of reasoning about parity classes directly.
        from itertools import permutations
        seen = set()
        frontier = [s1]
        seen.add(s1)
        while frontier:
            nxt = []
            for s in frontier:
                chars = list(s)
                for i in (0, 1):
                    j = i + 2
                    swapped = chars[:]
                    swapped[i], swapped[j] = swapped[j], swapped[i]
                    t = ''.join(swapped)
                    if t not in seen:
                        seen.add(t)
                        nxt.append(t)
            frontier = nxt
        return s2 in seen
